regions.comment: sprite graphics — shared enemy/decoration sprites (column-major, 128 bytes per 4×32 slot)

vsync IRQ handler — entry path. Writes 12 entries from palette_top (&493F..&494A) to &FE21. The last 4 bytes of the table (&494B..&494E) are present but never written — entry 15 = white is set once by Loader2 and never overridden.

palette_top — only [0..11] are read by the IRQ; [12..15] are present but unused. DEFAULT bytes in the on-disk file are the scenario-1 palette (red/yellow). Scenarios 2-4 are loaded by Loader2 PROCL34 / PROCL56 / PROCL78 which `READ T%?&493F` 16 bytes each from DATA at lines 1160 / 1180 / 1200 (dispatched from L%=?&9D at lines 940-970).
palette_bottom — 12 effective entries used for the scoreboard zone. Always black/blue/cyan/white regardless of scenario. Last 4 bytes again unused.

User VIA T1 timer-IRQ entry — fires mid-frame to swap to palette_bottom for the scoreboard. After writing, checks zp_irq_enable: if zero, chains immediately; otherwise restores the saved old IRQ vector (uninstall path) and chains. The BNE/BEQ pair at &497A/&497C is a guaranteed branch — both target irq_chain_old.
irq_install — saves the prior IRQ1V (&0204/&0205) at zp &64/&65 then installs irq_palette_split as the new handler. Also primes User VIA T1, IER, ACR for the mid-frame timer.
trailing data — small lookup table(s), purpose TBD

comments.0x4900: Two-path IRQ handler. Common prologue saves A/X/Y. Tests User VIA IFR bit 7 (T1 IRQ pending) → if so, jumps to irq_palette_split_b for the bottom palette. Otherwise tests sysVIA IFR bit 7 (vsync); if not vsync, chains immediately. Vsync path re-primes UVIA T1 (BC34 cycles ≈ mid-frame), then writes palette_top[0..11] → &FE21.
